Demographics.Address: 2200 Clairmont Ave S, Apt 108, Birmingham, AL 35205

Medications.Note: Patient is not currently prescribed any medications. Hypertension is being monitored without pharmacological intervention per patient preference. Dr. Stubbs has discussed antihypertensive options. Patient uses OTC ibuprofen PRN for knee and back pain.
Medications.CPAPEquipment: ResMed AirSense 11 AutoSet CPAP. Pressure setting 8-12 cmH2O. Mask: ResMed P30i nasal pillow. Average nightly compliance 6.4 hours per night.

Condition.Name: Essential Hypertension, Stage 1
Condition.Notes: Patient declines antihypertensive medication at this time. Home BP monitoring log maintained. Average home readings 140/89. Lifestyle modification counseled including sodium restriction, increased physical activity.

Condition.Name: Obstructive Sleep Apnea, Moderate
Condition.Notes: AHI 22 on diagnostic polysomnography. CPAP initiated November 2021. Good adherence reported. Patient notes improved alertness on road. DME supplier: Restwell Medical Supply, Birmingham.

Condition.Name: Obesity, Class I
Condition.Notes: BMI 33.7 at most recent visit. Weight stable at 233-237 lbs over past 2 years. Patient counseled on dietary changes and activity. Sedentary occupation (long-haul trucking) noted as contributing factor.

Condition.Name: Bilateral Knee Stiffness, NOS
Condition.Notes: Patient reports bilateral knee stiffness, worse in the morning and after prolonged sitting in truck cab. No formal imaging performed. OTC ibuprofen provides relief. Referral to orthopedics deferred by patient preference.

VitalSigns.BloodPressureSide: Right arm, seated, after 5 minute rest

Panel.name: Lipid Panel
Panel.ClinicalNote: Dyslipidemia pattern. Statin therapy discussed. Patient declined at this time. Dietary counseling provided.

Panel.name: Glucose and Metabolic
Panel.ClinicalNote: HbA1c in prediabetes range. Borderline. Repeat in 6 months. Weight loss and dietary changes emphasized.

Immunization.Administrator: Birmingham Sleep and Pulmonary Associates - Nurse on duty

Procedure.Name: Diagnostic Polysomnography (Sleep Study)
Procedure.Facility: Birmingham Sleep and Pulmonary Associates, Birmingham AL
Procedure.Results: AHI: 22 events/hour (Moderate OSA). SpO2 nadir: 84%. REM AHI: 31. NREM AHI: 18. Total sleep time: 362 minutes. Sleep efficiency: 81%. CPAP titration recommended.
Procedure.FollowUp: CPAP prescribed and initiated November 2021. Annual follow-up with Dr. Cho.

Appointment.Location: Birmingham Sleep and Pulmonary Associates, 1201 11th Ave S, Suite 300, Birmingham, AL 35205
Appointment.Instructions: Bring CPAP unit for data download. Bring CPAP usage log if maintained.

Appointment.Type: Annual Physical Examination and Lab Review
Appointment.Location: Irondale Family Practice, 106 24th St S, Birmingham, AL 35205
Appointment.Instructions: Fasting labs ordered in advance. Fast 10 hours prior. Repeat lipid panel and HbA1c. Bring home BP log.
